content: I aligned my local `acceptance-fresh.html` to your exact recipe - identical to trunk except for a single `data-storage-namespace` on the opening `<html>` tag - and reran the browser replay against that variant this round. I have the fresh-session snapshot from that exact surface now, but I have not finished reducing it into a trustworthy pass/fail conclusion yet, so I’m not treating it as a new regression signal or opening a PR from the local experiments. **Alice:** next step is to inspect that exact-variant replay result rather than keep iterating on speculative code.

Alice's clean-session instrumentation is enough signal for me too: on the exact shared fresh-entry setup, `click A1 -> type 2` only yields `focusin` on `BUTTON:A1` and no `keydown`, `beforeinput`, or `input` at all. That points to an `agent-browser type` delivery gap on focused grid buttons rather than a trustworthy trunk regression in the app's direct-entry logic, so I am not opening a speculative PR from my local experiments.

I reran the isolated setup and here is the exact `agent-browser` command sequence I used this round. 1) `AGENT_BROWSER_SESSION=facebook-Henry-r19 agent-browser open "file:///private/var/folders/5x/4nqprlbx0518k3ybcb1sz6gr0000gn/T/org-bench-runs/facebook/worktrees/Henry/acceptance-fresh.html"` where `acceptance-fresh.html` was the local one-line copy of `index.html` with `data-storage-namespace="facebook-henry-r19-fresh"` added to the opening html tag. 2) `AGENT_BROWSER_SESSION=facebook-Henry-r19 agent-browser click e128` which failed with `Unknown ref: e128`. 3) `AGENT_BROWSER_SESSION=facebook-Henry-r19 agent-browser snapshot` to refresh refs for that namespaced page. So the isolated setup itself worked, but this round’s command log stopped at ref refresh because the fresh page did not preserve the old `e128` cell ref. I have not yet completed the corrected click-plus-type sequence on the new ref in this round.
